module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 3: Freight Order Creation & Load Consolidation

learningObjectives.title: Analyze Freight Units for consolidation
learningObjectives.description: Evaluate demand lines for grouping opportunities before Freight Order creation.

learningObjectives.title: Apply capacity and route constraints
learningObjectives.description: Use truck limits and route compatibility to determine feasible load plans.

learningObjectives.title: Create optimized Freight Orders
learningObjectives.description: Build cost-effective orders without violating legal or operational constraints.

learningObjectives.title: Handle production planning failures
learningObjectives.description: Investigate and correct over-capacity or wrong-route consolidation issues.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 3: Freight Order Creation & Load Consolidation

sections.content: Blue Dart has been selected as the carrier in Lab 2. Before creating the Freight Order, three additional customer orders arrive. As the SAP TM Consultant, you must determine whether the shipments should be consolidated into one truck or transported separately while satisfying delivery commitments and minimizing transportation costs.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are responsible for load planning and transportation optimization. Your decision directly affects freight cost, truck utilization and customer service levels.

The planner proposes loading all four deliveries into one truck because the total weight is 17 tons. However, the truck can carry only 16 tons. Additionally, the Vellore delivery requires a different transportation route than the Chennai deliveries.

A junior planner creates a single Freight Order containing all four deliveries. During execution, the warehouse reports that the truck exceeds its legal weight limit and cannot be dispatched. Transportation operations are delayed.

sections.title: Consultant Investigation
rows: No, due to capacity and route constraints.
How many Freight Orders should be created?

sections.content: SAP TM should consolidate the Chennai Freight Units into one optimized Freight Order because they share the same destination region and delivery window. The Vellore shipment should be assigned to a separate Freight Order due to its different route. If the combined Chennai load still exceeds truck capacity, either split the load across two vehicles or assign a larger vehicle based on available resources.

sections.title: Consultant Best Practice
sections.content: Never consolidate shipments based only on destination. Always evaluate weight, volume, loading capacity, delivery window, route compatibility, dangerous goods requirements and vehicle restrictions before approving a Freight Order.

sections.title: Real Production Incident
sections.content: A logistics company consolidated six customer deliveries into one truck without validating axle-weight restrictions. The truck was stopped during a highway inspection, resulting in delivery delays and financial penalties. The issue was resolved by updating SAP TM planning constraints to enforce legal weight limits.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario Question
sections.content: A planner wants to merge all Freight Units into one Freight Order to reduce transportation cost. Would you approve it? Explain your decision. A consultant should discuss capacity utilization, routing, delivery windows, legal restrictions and optimization objectives before approving consolidation.

items: Two Freight Orders created based on route and capacity.
Vehicle utilization improved while maintaining delivery commitments.
Project continues to Lab 4: Warehouse Loading Delay & SAP EWM Integration.
